title: Verdant Analysis Request (v0)
description: The self-contained input the web tier enqueues for the R compute kernel. The kernel is stateless (ADR-0001): everything a fit needs is here. The request carries the breeder's INTENT, never a model specification — the kernel owns model choice and explains it back in the result bundle (ADR-0002). See contracts/README.md and DOMAIN-MODEL §6.

contract_version.description: Pins the message to this schema version. Additive within a major version; breaking changes bump the major and live in a new vN/ directory.

analysis_request_id.description: Optional caller-supplied id, echoed in the result bundle for correlation. Opaque to the kernel.

intent.description: What the breeder is analyzing FOR. The kernel turns intent + data into a model choice: 'selection' -> genotype random -> BLUPs; 'comparison' -> genotype fixed -> BLUEs; 'prediction' -> uses the relationship structure (A/G/H) to predict un/under-tested material. The web tier MUST NOT send a formula. It MAY send `model_overrides` naming preferred values for decisions the planner makes; the planner validates each against data readiness and may refuse an infeasible one with a reason (ADR-0002 / ADR-0018).

scope.description: Deliberate data-scoping metadata (DOMAIN-MODEL §2.2 discovery isolation). What material feeds a model is an expert choice, not an accident of the table. Informational in v0; the web tier is responsible for actually filtering observations before sending.

segment_id.description: The target Segment this analysis serves, if any. null = no commercial segment (e.g. discovery/unadapted screening), which excludes it from segment-pipeline training sets.

stage.description: The pipeline Stage this analysis pertains to, if scoped. Program-defined ordinal label.

is_discovery.description: True for discovery/unadapted-material trials; signals the result should not feed segment-pipeline predictions.

variables.description: The ObservationVariables (Trait × Method × Scale, BrAPI-Phenotyping) referenced by observations. Declares which are to be analyzed and how each is treated.

variable_id.description: Stable id used as the key in observations.

data_type.description: Drives how the kernel can model the variable. MVP analyzes 'numeric'.

analyze.description: Whether this variable is a response to be fit. False = present for context/gating only.

observation_units.description: The plots/plants (BrAPI ObservationUnit). Each carries its germplasm reference and its AS-PLANTED layout position — the actual physical arrangement, gaps and all (ADR-0006). The kernel uses these coordinates for spatial correction.

observation_unit_id.description: Stable id; the join key for observations.

germplasm_id.description: The genotype/entry on this unit (BrAPI Germplasm). Multiple units share a germplasm_id across reps/environments.

environment_id.description: Which environment (Location × Season) this unit belongs to. Required for MET/GxE; null/constant for single-trial.

layout.description: As-planted physical position. Provide row/col (and optionally range/pass) for spatial models. Absent or partial layout falls back to non-spatial analysis (the kernel decides and says so).

range.description: Breeder-casual synonym some programs use for row-blocks; carried through for as-planted fidelity.

pass.description: Breeder-casual synonym some programs use for the orthogonal direction.

observations.description: Long format: one entry per (observation_unit × variable) measured value. The kernel pivots as needed. Missing combinations are simply absent (unbalanced data is expected and handled).

value.description: The measured value. Numeric for quantitative traits; string for categorical; null for explicit missing.

design.description: Design HINTS, not a model spec. What the breeder believes the design was; the kernel validates against the actual layout and chooses the model. Optional — the kernel can infer from layout (ADR-0002/0006).

declared_design.description: What the breeder says the design is. The kernel treats this as a hint to confirm, not an instruction.

is_multi_environment.description: Hint that this is a MET (genotypes across environments). The kernel confirms from environment_id counts.

relationship.description: The relationship structure among genotypes — a FIRST-CLASS engine input, not a fork (DOMAIN-MODEL §6). A/G/H BLUP are one model with different relationship matrices (Mrode; Bernardo). MVP fills 'identity'.

type.description: identity = no kinship (MVP); A = pedigree-based; G = marker-based; H = single-step (pedigree+markers). A/G/H are MAPPED, not built — present so the seam anticipates M5–M6 without a contract break. Superseded by `model_overrides.relationship` when present (the authoritative override channel).

source_ref.description: For A/G/H: a reference the kernel resolves to the matrix or its inputs (pedigree set / marker set). Unused for 'identity'. Transport of large matrices is an OPEN operational detail (see README).

model_overrides.description: Optional breeder overrides of the planner's RECOMMENDED model decisions (ADR-0002 evolved / ADR-0018). NOT a model spec: each field names a decision the planner already makes and the breeder's preferred value. R remains the authority — the planner validates each override against data readiness and MAY refuse an infeasible one, keeping its recommendation and recording the reason. Absent = accept every recommendation. `model_overrides.relationship` is the authoritative relationship channel (supersedes the top-level `relationship.type`).

spatial.description: Force within-environment spatial de-trending on ('spats') or off ('none'). Refused if no environment has a usable row×col grid.

staging.description: Force a one-stage joint fit or a two-stage (Stage-1 de-trend → Stage-2 cross-env) fit. single_stage refused above the engine's equation budget.

gxe.description: Force a genotype×environment term in ('include') or fold it into the residual ('skip'). include refused without cross-environment connectivity + within-cell replication, or in a two-stage genotype-main fit.

relationship.description: Force the relationship structure. A needs a pedigree; G needs markers; H needs both. Refused when the required data is absent.

engine.description: Force the compute engine. Native BLUPF90 genomic (GBLUP/ssGBLUP) is Phase-2 and currently refused; genomic prediction runs on rrBLUP.

data_overrides.description: The breeder's raw-data SELECTION for this analysis (ADR-0021) — the data sibling of `model_overrides`. An analysis-scoped exclusion overlay: a filter list applied before the fit, NEVER a deletion of stored observations. Excluding data is the sole channel by which a data choice changes the model (drop a site → connectivity changes → the planner re-plans). Each re-run is a new immutable analysis; with-vs-without is a comparison, not a destruction. Absent = use all data.

exclusions.description: What to leave out of this fit. Findings (pre- or post-fit) propose exclusions; the breeder's disposition policy (review/batch/auto, with a per-trait cap) turns proposals into these entries.

kind.description: Exclusion level: drop a whole site, a single plot, or a whole entry. NOTE: there is deliberately no 'variable' (trait) level — choosing which traits enter the analysis is a SELECTION decision expressed through the objective's index weights (a weight of 0 drops a trait from the ranking), not a data-exclusion overlay (ADR-0021).

id.description: The environment_id / observation_unit_id / germplasm_id to exclude.

variable_id.description: Scope the exclusion to one trait (e.g. drop this plot's value for yield only). Null = the target's data across all traits.

reason.description: Why excluded — shown in the audit and the with/without comparison.

source.description: How this exclusion was created: a breeder click (manual), batch-accept of all suggestions (batch), or a standing auto-apply rule (auto_policy). Audit of how each exclusion happened (ADR-0021).

objective.description: The selection objective derived from the target Segment's TPP and the (Segment × Stage) SelectionCriteria (DOMAIN-MODEL §2.4). Drives gating and the index(es). Optional: absent for pure comparison/prediction runs.

gates.description: Independent culling thresholds on must-have traits (DOMAIN-MODEL §2.4). A candidate failing any gate is excluded regardless of index. Gates tighten with Stage.

threshold.description: Number for quantitative gates; string/array for categorical (e.g. accepted fruit types).

index_weights.description: Per-trait selection modes + weights for the selection index. Used by the transparent weighted index (sum-to-100% alignment tool) and, with the genetic covariance the kernel estimates, the Smith–Hazel / desired-gains index (ADR-0006).

mode.description: Selection mode for this trait (ADR-0006). 'max' = higher is better (merit = +z); 'min' = lower is better (merit = −z); 'target' = an optimum where deviation from `target` is penalized quadratically (merit = −((value−target)/sd)², in genotype-sd units, so far-from-target genotypes are increasingly discounted). Authoritative when present. If absent, the kernel derives it from `direction` (+1→max, −1→min), defaulting to 'max'.

target.description: The optimum value, on the trait's own scale, for mode='target'. Required when mode='target'; ignored for max/min.

direction.description: Legacy shorthand for max/min: +1 = higher is better, −1 = lower is better. Retained for back-compat and as the weights_used echo; `mode` supersedes it and is preferred for new producers. Not used for mode='target'.

weight.description: Relative importance. For the transparent index the web tier may normalize weights to sum to 1 (100%) as an alignment device.

desired_gain.description: Optional target gain for a desired-gains index (alternative elicitation to economic weights). The index-screen default — desired-gains vs economic weights — is an OPEN product detail (ADR-0006).

options.description: Non-scientific execution knobs. Nothing here may change WHICH model is chosen — that is the kernel's job (ADR-0002).

compute_genetic_correlations.description: Whether to estimate the genetic-correlation matrix across analyzed traits (M1+).

compute_stability.description: Whether to compute GxE/stability views for MET (M1+).
